key.converter.description: Key converter. Use StringConverter for plain string keys, JsonConverter for JSON keys. Default JsonConverter will fail if message keys are not valid JSON.
key.converter.note: Most common failure cause: default JsonConverter cannot deserialize non-JSON keys

value.converter.description: Value converter. JsonConverter for JSON messages, AvroConverter for Avro with Schema Registry, StringConverter for plain text.

value.converter.schemas.enable.description: Set to false if JSON messages don't include schema wrapper ({schema:{},payload:{}} format). Set to true only if messages follow the Kafka Connect JSON envelope format.

offset.flush.interval.ms.description: Interval in milliseconds at which to commit offsets. Lower values provide better exactly-once guarantees at the cost of more frequent offset commits.

topics.description: Comma-separated list of Kafka topics to consume from.

s3.bucket.name.description: S3 bucket name where data will be written.

s3.region.description: AWS region of the S3 bucket.

storage.class.description: Storage implementation class. Always use S3Storage for S3 Sink.

format.class.description: Output format class. Options: JsonFormat, AvroFormat, ParquetFormat, ByteArrayFormat.

flush.size.description: Number of records per partition before flushing to S3. Lower values = more frequent smaller files, higher values = fewer larger files.
flush.size.performance_impact: Directly affects file size and S3 PUT frequency. flush.size=1000 with 150B records ≈ 150KB files.

rotate.interval.ms.description: Time-based rotation interval in milliseconds. Files are flushed after this interval even if flush.size is not reached.
rotate.interval.ms.performance_impact: Ensures data freshness. Lower values = more frequent S3 writes.

partitioner.class.description: Partitioner class controlling S3 directory structure. DefaultPartitioner uses Kafka partition. TimeBasedPartitioner for time-based directories.

topics.dir.description: Top-level directory in S3 bucket. Files are written to {topics.dir}/{topic}/{partition}/.

s3.compression.type.description: Compression type for S3 files: none or gzip. Gzip reduces storage cost (~60-80% savings) but increases CPU usage.

rotate.schedule.interval.ms.description: Time-based rotation using wall-clock time (scheduled rotation). Unlike rotate.interval.ms which uses record timestamps.

s3.wan.mode.description: Use S3 Transfer Acceleration endpoint for cross-region writes.

s3.path.style.access.enabled.description: Use path-style S3 access instead of virtual-hosted style. Required for some S3-compatible stores.

s3.proxy.url.description: S3 proxy URL for environments requiring proxy access.

s3.part.size.description: S3 multipart upload part size in bytes. Minimum 5MB (5242880).

s3.retry.backoff.ms.description: Backoff time in milliseconds before retrying a failed S3 request.

s3.part.retries.description: Maximum number of retries for a failed S3 multipart upload part.

s3.http.send.expect.continue.description: Enable Expect: 100-Continue header for S3 PUT requests.

s3.canned.acl.description: S3 canned ACL to apply to uploaded objects (e.g., bucket-owner-full-control, private).

s3.ssea.name.description: S3 server-side encryption algorithm: AES256 or aws:kms.

s3.sse.kms.key.id.description: KMS key ID for aws:kms server-side encryption.

s3.credentials.provider.class.description: AWS credentials provider class. Use default for IAM Role-based auth (recommended).

store.kafka.keys.description: Include Kafka message keys in the output files.

store.kafka.headers.description: Include Kafka message headers in the output files.

keys.format.class.description: Format class for writing keys when store.kafka.keys=true.

headers.format.class.description: Format class for writing headers when store.kafka.headers=true.

behavior.on.null.values.description: How to handle null values: fail (throw error), ignore (skip record), or write (as tombstone marker).

s3.object.tagging.description: Enable S3 object tagging with offset metadata.

json.decimal.format.description: Format for decimal types in JSON output: BASE64 or NUMERIC.

format.bytearray.extension.description: File extension for ByteArrayFormat output files.

format.bytearray.separator.description: Separator between records in ByteArrayFormat output.

schema.compatibility.description: Schema compatibility mode: NONE, BACKWARD, FORWARD, FULL.

enhanced.avro.schema.support.description: Enable enhanced Avro schema support for complex types.

connect.meta.data.description: Include Connect metadata (topic, partition, offset) in output records.

avro.codec.description: Avro compression codec: null, deflate, snappy, bzip2, xz, zstandard.

s3.compression.level.description: Compression level for gzip. -1 for system default, 0-9 for specific levels.

timestamp.extractor.description: Timestamp extractor class for TimeBasedPartitioner: Wallclock, Record, RecordField.

timestamp.field.description: Field name for RecordField timestamp extractor.

directory.delim.description: Directory delimiter for S3 object keys.

file.delim.description: File delimiter in S3 object key names.

schema.partition.affix.type.description: Schema partition affix type: NONE, PREFIX, SUFFIX.

confluent_parameter_mapping.notes: Confluent Cloud S3 Sink uses simplified parameter names. The open-source version parameters listed above are compatible. Parameters not listed in the mapping are Confluent-only and do not apply.
